name: Coeliac Disease
diet_impact: Gluten triggers immune response and intestinal damage in the small intestine, impairing nutrient absorption.

general_rules: 100% exclusion of gluten from wheat, barley, rye and non-certified oats.
emphasise_foods: Naturally gluten-free whole foods.
Adequate protein intake to maintain muscle mass.
Healthy fats for gut healing and calorie sufficiency.
never_recommend: Wheat, barley, rye and non-certified oats.
Standard pasta, bread, cakes, most breakfast cereals, many sauces and ready meals containing gluten.

name: Crohn's Disease
diet_impact: Inflammation anywhere along the GI tract; high fibre and high-fat foods can worsen flares and symptoms.

general_rules: Maintain adequate protein with lower fat content.
emphasise_foods: Refined grains like white rice, rice pasta, potatoes, oats without seeds or nuts.
Cooked/peeled fruits and vegetables.
Lean proteins (eggs, white meat poultry, fish, tofu).
Non-caffeinated, non-alcoholic fluids.
never_recommend: Very high-fibre whole grains during flares.
Raw cruciferous vegetables and tough raw veg.
Very spicy foods with heavy garlic/onion.

name: Ulcerative Colitis
diet_impact: Colon and rectum inflammation; high fibre and insoluble residues can worsen flares and urgency.

general_rules: Limit insoluble fibre, seeds and tough skins.
Avoid caffeine, alcohol and very spicy foods.
emphasise_foods: Refined grains (white rice, white bread, plain crackers).
Ripe or canned fruits without skins or seeds.
Well-cooked, non-cruciferous vegetables.
Lean proteins and modest low-fat dairy if tolerated.
never_recommend: High-fibre whole grains (brown rice, quinoa, whole wheat, barley).
Raw cruciferous vegetables and dried fruits.
Very spicy foods, caffeine and alcohol.

name: Irritable Bowel Syndrome
diet_impact: Symptoms are often triggered by fermentable carbohydrates (FODMAPs).

general_rules: Prefer low-FODMAP fruits, vegetables and grains.
Reintroduce triggers slowly based on tolerance.
emphasise_foods: Low-FODMAP fruits (bananas, berries, citrus, kiwi).
Low-FODMAP vegetables (carrots, courgette, aubergine, spinach).
Lactose-free or plant-based milks.
Gluten-free or low-FODMAP grains.
never_recommend: Wheat-based breads and pastas during strict phase.
Apples, pears, stone fruits and many beans.

name: Pancreatitis (Acute/Chronic)
diet_impact: Fat intake drives pancreatic enzyme secretion; high-fat foods and alcohol exacerbate pain and inflammation.

general_rules: Very low-fat diet (often 30–50 g per day in chronic disease).
Small, frequent meals rather than large ones.
Emphasise lean protein and complex carbohydrates.
emphasise_foods: Skinless poultry, lean meats, white fish, egg whites.
Low-fat or non-fat dairy if tolerated, or suitable alternatives.
Whole grains and fruits/vegetables not prepared with added fats.

name: Type 2 Diabetes
diet_impact: Carbohydrates and overall energy intake strongly influence blood glucose and insulin resistance.

general_rules: Limit total carbohydrates per meal (commonly 45–60 g, personalised).
Prefer low-GI carbs and high-fibre foods.
emphasise_foods: Non-starchy vegetables.
High-fibre whole grains in moderate portions.
Lean protein and healthy fats to blunt glucose spikes.
never_recommend: Huge portions of white rice, white bread or potatoes.
